name: EditorTools
description: A comprehensive collection of toolbar button components for TipTap-based rich text editors. Provides formatting controls including text styling (bold, italic, strikethrough), headings, lists, code blocks, blockquotes, images, and history management (undo/redo). Each tool button integrates with TipTap editor instance and shows active states.
usage_guidelines: Use with RichTextEditor or any TipTap-based editor implementation.
Pass the TipTap editor instance to each tool button component.
Compose custom toolbars by selecting specific tool buttons needed.
Tool buttons automatically show active states based on current selection.
Tool buttons are automatically disabled when actions are not available.
Group related tools logically in toolbar (e.g., text formatting, lists, blocks).
Use ToolButton base component for creating custom editor tools.
Handle image uploads with onUpload callback in ToolButtonImage.

base_component.name: ToolButton
base_component.description: Base toolbar button component with active/disabled states and hover effects

active.description: Shows active state with background highlight

variant.description: Button variant - 'input' for file upload buttons

headingLevel.description: Array of heading levels to show in dropdown

ToolButtonHeading.note: Renders as dropdown with options for H1-H6 and Paragraph

ToolButtonCodeBlock.description: Code block formatting with syntax highlighting

ToolButtonImage.description: Insert image with file upload support

onUpload.description: Callback for handling image file uploads

ToolButtonImage.note: Converts uploaded images to base64 by default. Use onUpload to handle custom upload logic.

editor.description: TipTap editor instance from useEditor hook. All tool buttons require this prop.

BaseToolButtonProps.description: Base props for all tool buttons

styling_info.visual_description: Toolbar buttons with icons, rounded corners, and padding. Active state shows gray background highlight. Hover effects on non-disabled buttons. Disabled buttons have reduced opacity. Consistent sizing (text-lg, p-1.5) across all buttons.

Do: Always pass valid TipTap editor instance to tool buttons
Group related tools together in toolbar (formatting, lists, blocks, history)
Use Divider or spacing to separate tool groups visually
Provide tooltips via title prop for better UX
Handle image uploads with custom onUpload callback for server storage
Use ToolButtonHeading with appropriate heading levels for your content
Include undo/redo buttons for better user experience
Test tool button states with different editor content
Use consistent icon sizes across custom tools
Dont: Don't use without TipTap editor instance
Don't overload toolbar with too many tools (prioritize common actions)
Don't forget to handle tool state updates
Don't use for non-TipTap editor contexts
Don't modify editor state outside of tool button commands
Don't forget to configure required TipTap extensions for tools
Don't use ToolButtonImage without handling large file uploads properly

examples.title: Basic Toolbar
Full Featured Toolbar
Custom Tool Button

required_extensions.description: TipTap extensions required for tool buttons to work
required_extensions.StarterKit: Includes most basic formatting (bold, italic, strike, headings, lists, blockquote, code, codeBlock, hardBreak, horizontalRule, history)
required_extensions.Highlight: Required if using custom highlight tool
required_extensions.TextAlign: Required if using text alignment tools

integration_notes: All tool buttons use TipTap's chain() API for command execution
Active states are determined by editor.isActive() method
Disabled states use editor.can() to check command availability
Tool buttons automatically focus editor before executing commands
Image tool converts files to base64 by default - implement custom upload for production
Heading tool renders as dropdown with configurable levels
All buttons support standard HTML button props (onClick, className, etc.)

AllTools.title: All Tools
